Intel® Core™ Architecture · Generation Eventide · 2028
EVENTIDE
Core Ultra 500 / Xeon 500 Series

The most architecturally ambitious APU Intel has ever built. Eventide doesn't iterate — it redefines the boundary between compute, graphics, AI, signal processing, and power at the process node level. A spiritual successor to Lunar Lake's efficiency ethos and Panther Lake's performance ambition, Eventide collapses six specialized silicon eras into a single, vertically integrated package.

Intel 04A · 0.4nm Intel 06E · 0.6nm Intel 14A-E FOveros 2.0 Photonic V8 HyperBOOST Arc Elementalist Xe5 256 TOPS NPU World-First AV2 Encode
Silicon Foundation
Four process nodes.
One package.

Eventide is the first consumer chip to leverage three distinct in-house Intel process nodes and a photonic tile interconnect simultaneously. Every tile is assigned its optimal manufacturing process — delivering performance, efficiency, and cost advantages that no monolithic design could achieve.

Intel 04A
Half-Nanometer Performance Node
The most advanced transistor node Intel has ever mass-produced
  • Process generation0.4nm-class GAA w/ GaNSi gate material
  • Transistor architectureGate-All-Around nanosheet, 4-stack
  • GaNSi advantageThermal conductivity 3× Si, near-zero tunnelling at sub-0.5nm
  • Interconnect metalRuthenium local interconnect, MoSi² contact
  • Power deliveryPowerVia 2.0 backside PDN + Hybrid Bonding
  • 3D stackingAdvanced Stacked Silicon Interconnect (ASSI) 2.0 — 0.4µm pitch hybrid bond
  • Voltage scaling0.35V min operating; sub-threshold Vt control ±2mV
  • Heat distributionGaNSi substrate lateral thermal spreading; peak 850 W/m·K
  • Tiles built on 04ACompute, Kache Kore™, iGPU (Arc E1080), HNPU

Intel 06E
Efficiency-First Node
Ground-up design for Performance Per Watt, not raw clocks
  • Process generation0.6nm-class FinFlex GAA, efficiency-biased Vt
  • Design philosophyIPC and PPW maximized; frequency ceiling deliberately soft-capped
  • Leakage controlUltra-low standby leakage: <0.3nA/µm gate leakage
  • Clock networkMulti-Vt adaptive body biasing; resonant clock mesh
  • Power deliveryPowerVia 1.5 backside PDN; granular per-cluster DVFS
  • Idle power stateClockgate + power gate in <1ns; sub-µW idle per cluster
  • IPC advantage+22% IPC uplift vs 14A at same frequency
  • Tiles built on 06ELow Power Island (UHE+LPE), LPiGPU (Arc Druid), QuickSync BionzXR, LPNPU

FOveros 2.0
FiberOptic-VEROS Photonic Interconnect
World-first photonic tile interconnect in a consumer package
  • Co-developed withLightmatter USA (photonic interconnect IP)
  • Interconnect mediumSilicon photonic waveguides; on-package optical mesh
  • Bandwidth (tile-to-tile)Up to 448 Tbps aggregate photonic bandwidth
  • Latency vs copper~70% lower tile-to-tile signaling latency vs Foveros 1.0 copper
  • Signal power~0.08 pJ/bit vs ~2.5 pJ/bit electrical at equivalent bandwidth
  • ModulationPAM-8 silicon photonic ring modulators; 1310nm wavelength
  • Electrical fallbackHybrid copper+photonic; copper used for low-latency control paths
  • GPU multi-tile syncEnables 4× E1080 tiles to present as single render device via hardware arbitration
448
Tbps photonic BW
−70%
Tile-to-tile latency
×31
Signal efficiency vs Cu
Photonic Tile Interconnect · Co-developed with Lightmatter USA
FOveros 2.0
Photonic
Lightmatter USA Silicon Photonic IP World-First Consumer PAM-8 · 1310nm

FOveros 2.0 — FiberOptic-VEROS — is the result of a deep engineering collaboration between Intel and Lightmatter USA, the photonic interconnect IP company. Every tile-to-tile data path inside Eventide runs on silicon photonic waveguides, not copper. Light replaces wire. The result: 448 Tbps of aggregate bandwidth at 0.08 pJ/bit — a 31× efficiency gain over equivalent electrical links. And because photons travel faster through the waveguide medium than electrons through copper at scale, tile-to-tile signaling latency drops by ~70%. This is what makes the 4× E1080 GPU multi-tile configuration possible — and what makes Kache Kore™ an effective bLLC for the entire SoC at once.

448
Tbps aggregate photonic bandwidth
−70%

pJ/bit (vs ~2.5 pJ/bit electrical)

PAM-8 ring modulators · 1310 nm wavelength
Copper hybrid fallback for low-latency control paths

4× E1080 MULTI-TILE UNIFICATION

FOveros 2.0 photonic arbitration + HW Thread Director allow four discrete Arc E1080 tiles to present as a single render device to drivers and games — unified command queues, unified framebuffer. Impossible with copper interconnects at this scale.

Core Architecture
Five core types.
One unified vision.

Eventide introduces the most segmented core architecture in x86 history — each core type purpose-built for a specific performance regime. Complexity is managed by the dedicated Thread Director tile and FOveros 2.0 photonic scheduling bus, not software.

— COMPUTE TILE · INTEL 04A —
Compute Tile · UHP Core
SOLAR ECLIPSE
Ultra High Performance · P-core Evolution

The UHP is Intel's fastest-clocking x86 core ever. Built wide for heat dispersion, it sacrifices peak IPC density in favor of raw frequency headroom. V8 HyperBOOST allows octets of UHP cores to fire in sequential bursts using a Ford Mustang V8 cylinder-offset firing pattern, sustaining effective 10 GHz workloads across thermally aware hardware scheduling windows. No SMT — threads are power, not sharing.

Compute Tile · DP Core
SUNSET COVE
Dense Performance · SMT P-Core

The DP core is the IPC workhorse of Eventide — the highest IPC uplift of any core in this generation's lineup. It reintroduces Hyper-Threading to the P-core class (removed since Lion Cove) with a new 2-thread-per-core implementation featuring disjoint execution ports, minimizing thread contention. While it cannot match the UHP's raw clock ceiling, its instruction throughput per mm² is unmatched in the x86 landscape.

Compute Tile · SPE Core
VENUSMONT
SPammable E-Core · Extreme Density

Nine SPE cores fit in the same die footprint as a single UHP core. Designed for massively parallel workloads traditionally requiring server infrastructure: scientific compute, AI data prep, parallel compilation, simulation. The X500 SKU can house hundreds of SPE cores across four compute tiles, delivering server-grade thread counts in a mobile chassis.

Core density

— LOW POWER ISLAND · INTEL 06E —
Low Power Island · UHE Core
LUNAR ECLIPSE
Ultra High Efficiency · Next-Gen Lunar Lake Heir

The UHE is the spiritual successor to the Lunar Lake efficiency core — but now manufactured on Intel 06E, a purpose-built efficiency node. The result is a core that meets or exceeds last-generation P-core performance while operating at dramatically lower voltages. As in Lunar Lake's philosophy, the system preferentially delegates all workloads to UHE cores before escalating to the Compute Tile. The Low Power Island can sustain all typical productivity, browsing, streaming, and communication tasks indefinitely with the Compute tile fully powered off.

Low Power Island · LPE Core
DARKMONT
Low Power Efficiency · Rebinned E-Core on 06E

Darkmont takes the last-generation E-core architecture from Panther Lake and rebins it onto Intel 06E, achieving a dramatic reduction in operating power. At slight undervolting, LPE cores operate in fractions of a watt — enabling a world-first scenario where the system draws as little as 1–5W total under active light use. Video playback, Zoom calls, web browsing, and background tasks are all within the LPE's sustained operational envelope without touching the UHE or Compute Tile.

Kache Kore™

4GB bLLC / L4 cache on Intel 04A. Central cache for all tiles. Acts as cache-latency DRAM for the APU — eliminates DRAM round-trips for hot working sets.

V8 HyperBOOST 1.0

Octets of UHP cores fire in Ford Mustang V8 offset sequence. Hardware Thread Director pre-schedules burst windows. Sustains effective 10 GHz throughput for frequency-bound tasks within thermal budget.

Turbo Boost Max 5.0

Next evolution of Intel's binning-based boost algorithm. Continuous per-core voltage-frequency profiling. Feeds V8 HyperBOOST with real-time thermal headroom data.

Thread Director Tile

Dedicated silicon scheduler tile. Handles 5-class core arbitration, tile sleep/wake, GPU escalation, NPU offload decisions, and thermal budget allocation — all in hardware, at sub-100ns latency.

Cache Architecture · Intel 04A
Kache Kore™
How Intel Beats x3D

AMD's 3D V-Cache stacks extra SRAM on top of the CPU die. Kache Kore™ is Intel's counter-strike — a dedicated 4 GB beyond-Last-Level Cache tile built on Intel 04A, directly connected to every tile on the SoC via FOveros 2.0 photonic short-path. It's not just more cache — it's cache at a scale that functions as cache-latency DRAM for the entire APU. The 4 GB bLLC holds entire game working sets, eliminating DRAM round-trips entirely for hot data. Where AMD's x3D tops out at 192 MB, Kache Kore™ delivers over 4,000 MB — and it's shared across CPU, GPU, and NPU simultaneously.

AMD 3D V-CACHE
3D V-Cache
  • · 64 MB or 96 MB stacked SRAM
  • · CPU-only benefit
  • · Does not help GPU workloads
  • · High cost: stacked silicon process
  • · Fixed allocation, no dynamic sharing
INTEL KACHE KORE™ bLLC
Kache Kore™
  • · 4 GB bLLC — 40× AMD V-Cache size
  • · CPU, GPU, NPU all benefit
  • · Shared across all APU tiles via photonics
  • · Intel 04A — cost-efficient at 0.4nm
  • · HW Thread Director dynamically routes hot data
4 GB
bLLC on Intel 04A
×40
vs AMD 3D V-Cache size

APU-unified (CPU + GPU + NPU)
−70%
DRAM round-trips for hot worksets

  • 1% low frametime stabilityNear-eliminated stutter
  • Open world streamingFull zone held in bLLC
  • GPU framebuffer cachingbLLC reduces VRAM pressure
  • AI model inferenceModel weights cached in bLLC
Dedicated Scheduler Silicon · HW Thread Director
Thread Director
Unified Intelligence.

The Hardware Thread Director is not firmware. It is dedicated silicon — a purpose-built scheduling tile that operates independently of all CPU cores, making real-time arbitration decisions at sub-100ns latency. With Eventide's 5-class heterogeneous core architecture (UHP, DP, SPE, UHE, LPE) spanning two process nodes, software schedulers simply cannot respond fast enough. The Thread Director tile monitors every core's thermal headroom, instruction profile, and priority class simultaneously, and reassigns threads in hardware without OS intervention.

A new capability in Eventide: the HW Thread Director now operates in co-pilot mode with the LPNPU. The always-on Low Power NPU (88 TOPS, Intel 06E) runs a lightweight on-chip AI scheduling model that predicts workload class in advance — feeding predictive dispatch decisions to the Thread Director before threads even become runnable. The result: smarter core selection, fewer thermal missteps, and better sustained performance over time.

Open Thread Director Technology Page →
  • 🎯
    5-Class Core Arbitration
    Real-time assignment across UHP, DP, SPE (04A) and UHE, LPE (06E) based on instruction class, priority, and thermal head
  • 🧠
    LPNPU Co-Pilot (AI-Assisted Scheduling)
    Always-on LPNPU runs predictive workload classification — pre-routes threads to optimal core class before scheduling window opens
  • ⚡
    V8 HyperBOOST Sequencer
    Pre-schedules UHP burst firing windows using TBM 5.0 thermal data — coordinates the 1-4-6-3-8-5-7-2 offset sequence
  • 💡
    Tile Sleep / Wake Orchestration
    Powers down entire compute tiles in microseconds. Wakes GPU, NPU, BionzXR tiles on-demand — below OS visibility
  • 🔀
    GPU Escalation + NPU Offload
    Identifies AI and graphics tasks in CPU instruction stream; offloads to HNPU or Arc E1080 without driver round-trip
THREAD CLASSIFICATION → CORE DISPATCH
LATENCY
→ UHP Solar Eclipse (04A)
THROUGHPUT
→ DP Sunset Cove (04A)
PARALLEL
→ SPE Venusmont (04A)
BALANCED
→ UHE Lunar Eclipse (06E)
BACKGROUND
→ LPE Darkmont (06E)
AI TASK
→ HNPU/LPNPU (silicon offload)
GRAPHICS
→ Arc E1080 / Druid
LPNPU CO-PILOT

The LPNPU (88 TOPS, <1.4W, always-on) runs Intel's on-chip workload prediction model. It classifies upcoming instruction patterns and feeds pre-routing signals to the Thread Director — allowing proactive core preparation before threads become runnable.

  • Scheduling latency<100ns hardware
  • Core classes managed5 (UHP, DP, SPE, UHE, LPE)
  • Process nodeIntel 14A-E (scheduler logic)
  • LPNPU AI assistYes — predictive dispatch
  • OS dependencyNone — fully hardware-native

Graphics Architecture · Eventide
Three GPU architectures.
One for everything.

Eventide houses three distinct graphics architectures: Arc Elementalist for extreme performance, Arc Druid for sustained efficiency, and the 2D Kanvas Renderer for zero-cost desktop composition. Each operates on dedicated silicon — freeing every transistor to do exactly what it was designed for.

Intel® Arc · 1000 Series
Elementalist
Xe5 Architecture · Intel 04A · Performance iGPU

The fifth-generation Xe architecture — the widest shader core, deepest wave dispatch, and first native-FP64 execution unit Intel has ever shipped. XMX5 matrix acceleration with sub-byte INT2/FP4 precision. RTU5 ray tracing with BVH4 traversal and VR stereo BVH caching. Up to four tiles fused via FOveros 2.0 photonic fabric into one GPU — the first iGPU to decisively surpass dedicated desktop flagships.

  • Shader generationXe5 · 5th-gen unified shader · 2.2× IPC over Xe2
  • AI computeXMX5 · INT2 / FP4 / INT8 / FP8 / BF16 native
  • Ray tracingRTU5 · BVH4 · ReSTIR · VR stereo BVH
  • ProcessIntel 04A · FOveros 2.0 photonic · up to ×4 tiles
  • Featured GPUArc E1080 · see GPU Lineup section ↓
vs XE2 (BATTLEMAGE)
Shader IPC
+120%
AI TOPS/unit
+340%
RT rays/clock
+200%
Intel® Arc · Eventide LP
Druid
Xe4E Architecture · Intel 06E · Efficiency LP iGPU

A purpose-built efficiency variant of the Xe4 desktop architecture, re-fabbed on Intel 06E. Per-shader power gating — each EU powers off completely when idle, not just clock-gated. Adaptive clock stepping adjusts voltage-frequency per-core every 100μs. XMX4E GPU matrix acceleration for high-throughput Windows AI workloads. Hardware RTU4 ray tracing at LP TDPs. 3W sustained active floor.

  • Shader generationXe4E · per-shader power gating · 3W floor
  • AI computeXMX4E · GPU-coupled · ~553 TOPS (D390)
  • Ray tracingRTU4 · hardware BVH · area-light sampling
  • ProcessIntel 06E · −35% power/transistor vs 04A
  • Featured GPUArc D390 · see GPU Lineup section ↓
vs PANTHER LAKE iGPU
Perf-per-watt
+58%
AI TOPS (always-on)
+280%
Active power floor
−62%
Intel® · Ancillary Tile
2D Kanvas Renderer
2DKR · Intel 14A-E · Dedicated 2D Silicon

Purpose-built silicon for all desktop GUI composition. Zero 3D ALUs. Zero shaders. Zero Z-buffers. The 2DKR handles every pixel you see when you're not gaming — windows, taskbar, overlays, scrolling — at sub-millisecond latency and under a watt. During gaming, it renders all 2D overlays with zero GPU overhead, freeing the Arc Elementalist and Druid entirely for 3D workloads.

  • ArchitectureModernised Blitter Engine · scanline pipeline
  • 3D capabilityNone — pure 2D acceleration
  • Power<1W active · <0.1W idle · 0W GPU overhead
  • ProcessIntel 14A-E · ~4 mm²
  • RoleDesktop composition · game overlays · API routing

AI Rendering Technology · Powered by HNPU + Xe5
XeSS 5.0
NEXT IN AI RENDERING

Eventide's Xe5 architecture and 256-TOPS HNPU combine to deliver Intel's most advanced AI rendering suite yet — XeSS 5.0 upscaling, XeFG 3.0 frame generation, Xe-MFG 2.0 multi-frame generation, and the new XeLL Pro+ latency compensation system. Together they transform raw GPU performance into the highest-quality, lowest-latency gaming experience on any mobile platform.

HNPU-Accelerated Xe5 Architecture 4× Frame Multiplier
XeSS 5.0 · AI UPSCALING
XeSS 5.0
Xe Super Sampling — 5th Generation

5th-gen AI upscaler powered by HNPU XMX5. Near-native image quality at Performance preset. Temporal stability improvements rival native rendering in motion clarity and fine detail.

Quality modeNear-native IQ
Perf upliftUp to 3.5× framerate
AccelerationHNPU XMX5 units

XeFG 3.0 · FRAME GENERATION
XeFG 3.0
Xe Frame Generation — 3rd Generation

AI frame interpolation generating synthetic frames between rendered frames. 3rd-gen model improves motion handling and ghosting elimination by 60% vs XeFG 2.0.

MultiplierUp to 2× per frame
w/ XeSS combinedUp to 7× effective fps

Xe-MFG 2.0 · MULTI-FRAME
Xe-MFG 2.0
Xe Multi-Frame Generation — 2nd Generation

Generates multiple AI frames per single rendered output. Combined with XeSS 5.0, achieves up to 4× frame multiplication — the most aggressive frame gen Intel has shipped.

Max multiplier4× (with XeSS 5.0)
AI engineHNPU + Xe5 XMX5
Min native fps30 fps for quality
Frames generated2–4 per render
XeLL Pro+ · LATENCY
XeLL Pro+
Xe Low Latency Pro+

Anti-latency system with hardware frame warp. Reads mouse and controller input at the hardware level, shifts the completed frame in the direction of motion before it reaches the display, then runs an HNPU edge-regeneration pass to AI-inpaint the newly revealed screen borders — eliminating the perceptual lag introduced by frame generation entirely.

Frame shiftInput-driven motion warp
Edge regenHNPU AI border inpainting
Effective latency≤ native w/ 4× MFG
Works withXeFG 3.0 + Xe-MFG 2.0
Arc GPU Family · Performance Tiers
The Arc 1000 Series.
A new tier is born.

Intel Arc is structured around performance tiers from everyday to extreme. The 1000 Series is the first tier to leave dedicated desktop flagships behind — in every metric.

300
Entry Level
Everyday computing, streaming, and light gaming for thin-and-light platforms.
500
Midrange
Capable 1440p gaming and strong AI assist for mainstream performance laptops.
700
High End
4K gaming and ray tracing headroom. Near-dedicated GPU performance for premium content creation.
1000
Elementalist · Xe5 · Enthusiast
The first Arc tier to decisively surpass dedicated desktop flagships. Xe5 shader architecture, XMX5 matrix acceleration, and RTU5 ray tracing on Intel 04A — the world's most advanced mobile node. Up to ×4 FOveros tiles, unified as a single render device.
Xe5 Architecture XMX5 AI RTU5 Ray Tracing Intel 04A
Arc 1000 Series · GPU Lineup
Choose your
performance tier.

E-series carries Elementalist Xe5 from the flagship ×4-tile E1080 down to the budget E310. D-series carries Druid Xe4E for thermally-constrained LP mobile platforms.

Intel® Arc · E-Series · Flagship
E1080
Elementalist · Xe5 · Intel 04A · Up to ×4 Tiles

Purpose-built for multi-tile iGPU. A single E1080 tile delivers ~5× the raster performance of the RTX 5090 desktop. Four tiles on the X500 via FOveros 2.0 deliver ~20× RTX 5090 raster performance and ~67,830 combined AI TOPS.

  • Xe5-cores (per tile)220 · 112,640 shaders
  • XMX5 AI units (per tile)1,760 units · ~16,958 TOPS/tile
  • XMX5 combined (×4 tiles)7,040 units · ~67,830 TOPS
  • RTU5 (per tile)880 units
  • GPU boost clock2.5 GHz
  • Max tile config (X500)×4 · single render device
  • MemoryKache Kore™ L4 (4 GB bLLC) + ZAM (FOveros-mounted)
  • VR supportVRV1 / VRV2 · stereo BVH caching
  • APIDirectX 13, Vulkan 1.4, OpenCL 3.1

The flagship Druid efficiency GPU. RTX 5060M-class raster at 22W peak. Always-on in Eventide — handling sub-threshold GPU tasks without waking Elementalist tiles, keeping the system responsive at near-zero power cost.

  • Xe4E-cores14 · 3,584 shaders

  • GPU boost clock2.4 GHz
  • TDP range3W – 22W (adaptive)

  • PPW vs B390+58% performance-per-watt

Platform Architecture · Tile Mosaic
Every function.
Its own silicon.

Eventide is not a monolithic processor. It is an orchestrated mosaic of purpose-built tiles — each manufactured on the process node optimal for its function, each operating independently or in concert. From always-on neural inference to 16K display output to zero-latency audio — this is what it means to build a complete platform from the silicon up.

Intel 04A · 06E · 14A-E FOveros 2.0 Photonic Fabric 12 Discrete Tile Domains Multi-Collab Engineering
◆   AI Compute Tiles
High-Performance NPU · Intel 04A
HNPU
Neural Processing Unit · Intel 04A · Compute Tile

The flagship neural processor in the Eventide lineup. Built on Intel's most advanced node, the HNPU delivers 256 TOPS with full INT2 through FP32 precision coverage and 32 MB of tightly-coupled on-die scratchpad SRAM for model weight staging. Handles Copilot+ AI features, real-time XeSS 5.0, LLM inference, and heavy background AI workloads — coordinated with the LPNPU by the Hardware Thread Director.

  • AI Throughput256 TOPS
  • Precision ModesINT2/4/8, FP8, BF16, FP16, FP32
  • On-die SRAM32 MB scratchpad
  • FrameworksOpenVINO 5.0, ONNX, TFLite, PyTorch
  • ProcessIntel 04A
256
TOPS
32
MB SRAM
7
Precisions
Low-Power NPU · Intel 06E
LPNPU
Always-On Neural Processor · Intel 06E · LP Island

Eventide's always-on intelligence layer. Running at under 1.4W peak, the LPNPU never sleeps — monitoring ambient audio for wake-on-voice, co-piloting the Hardware Thread Director with predictive AI task dispatch, and running SONAR presence detection from WiFi signal analysis. Combined system TOPS reach 344 across both NPUs.

  • AI Throughput88 TOPS
  • Peak Power Draw< 1.4W at full TOPS
  • Precision ModesINT4, INT8, FP8, BF16
  • Key RolesWake-on-Voice · SONAR · TD Co-Pilot
  • ProcessIntel 06E
ALWAYS-ON344 TOPS COMBINED
Neural Accelerator · 14A-E
GNA 5.0
Gaussian & Neural Accelerator · Intel 14A-E

Intel's ultra-low-power speech and audio inference engine. Optimised for always-on Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) and shallow neural network workloads — noise suppression, keyword recognition, speaker identification, acoustic context detection — at near-zero CPU cost. Works in concert with the Klangkerne audio tile for a fully hardware-offloaded audio intelligence stack.

  • Primary UseSpeech, Noise, Audio AI
  • OperationsGMM + DNN, INT8, BF16
  • Power ModeSub-milliwatt idle inference
  • ProcessIntel 14A-E
◆   Media & Codec Tiles
High-Performance Media · Intel × Sony · 06E
QuickSync BionzXR
8-Core Camera-Grade Transcoder · Intel 06E · Co-developed with Sony Semiconductor

The world's first camera-grade media engine integrated in a consumer mobile processor. Co-engineered between Intel QuickSync and Sony Semiconductor's BionzXR imaging silicon teams — the same BIONZ lineage found in Sony's α-series and Cinema Line cameras — the BionzXR Cores bring broadcast-grade transcoding directly to the APU package.

Delivers world-first AV2 hardware encode, full ProRes hardware encode at 8K120, AV1 decode at 8K240, and native VRV1/VRV2 for VR video pipelines. Sony USB-C/Thunderbolt plug-and-play: connect an α-series camera and immediately stream or encode RAW frames with zero software overhead.

  • Cores (X500)8× BionzXR cores
  • World-FirstAV2 hardware encode
  • Encode Peak8K120 ProRes · 8K240 AV1
  • Encode CodecsAV2, AV1, H.266/VVC, H.265, H.264, VP9, ProRes, VRV1, VRV2
  • Decode CodecsAll encode + VP8, MPEG-4, VC-1
  • Camera IntegrationSony USB-C / TB5 plug-and-play RAW ISP
  • ProcessIntel 06E
INTEL × SONY SEMICONDUCTOR BIONZXR LINEAGE CAMERA-GRADE
LP Media Engine · Intel 14A-E
MFX QuickSync
Low-Power Decode Engine · Intel 14A-E · LP Island

Always-on low-power decode for streaming, playback, and background media tasks. Offloads BionzXR from routine decode work, freeing the full codec engine for encode and capture pipelines. Runs the entire video decode stack at up to 35% lower power than the previous-generation QuickSync on Panther Lake.

  • Decode CodecsAV2, AV1, H.266/VVC, H.265, H.264, VP9, VP8
  • Encode (limited)H.265, AV1, H.264
  • Power Uplift−35% vs PTL QuickSync
  • ProcessIntel 14A-E
◆   Vision & Imaging
Image Processing Unit · Intel × Sony · 14A-E
Intel IPU 8.2
201MP Camera Pipeline · Intel 14A-E · Sony Native Integration

Eventide's Image Processing Unit supports up to 201 megapixel camera input, with Sony α-series and Cinema Line plug-and-play via USB-C and Thunderbolt 5. The ISP pipeline includes RAW demosaic, multi-frame HDR tone mapping, multi-band noise reduction, and lens distortion correction — all with LPNPU co-processing for real-time scene classification and computational photography.

  • Max InputUp to 201 MP
  • Camera IntegrationSony USB-C / TB5 native
  • ISP PipelineRAW demosaic · HDR · NR · Lens correction
  • AI Co-processingLPNPU real-time scene AI
  • ProcessIntel 14A-E
INTEL × SONY SEMICONDUCTOR α-SERIES NATIVE
◆   Audio
Audio Engine · Intel × Bang & Olufsen · 14A-E
Klangkerne™
Intel® Klangkerne™ Audio Engine · Intel 14A-E · Co-developed with Bang & Olufsen

The first fully vertically integrated audio stack on a consumer APU. Where every prior PC audio solution stacked Intel silicon → Realtek codec → WinAPI/DirectSound with multiplying latency at each handoff, the Klangkerne collapses that chain to a single in-package tile, co-engineered with Bang & Olufsen's acoustic DSP and tuning teams.

Built on Intel 14A-E, the Klangkerne implements the Intel × B&O Sound API — a purpose-built audio runtime exposing hardware-direct pathways to the Eventide OS, with WinAPI and DirectSound compatibility headers for Windows installations. Supports Dolby Atmos, DTS:X, B&O Beosonic spatial sound tuning, and multi-zone audio routing.

  • ArchitectureVertically integrated — no external codec
  • ProcessIntel 14A-E
  • Spatial AudioDolby Atmos · DTS:X · B&O Beosonic
  • Driver StackIntel × B&O Sound API (native OS)
  • Compat. HeadersWinAPI · DirectSound (Windows mode)
  • Key Design GoalsLatency · Efficiency · Sound Excellence
INTEL × BANG & OLUFSEN BEOSONIC TUNING DOLBY ATMOS DTS:X
GNA 5.0 · Audio AI Path
GNA 5.0
Gaussian & Neural Accelerator · Intel 14A-E

The Klangkerne works in tight coupling with the GNA 5.0 to deliver a completely hardware-offloaded audio intelligence pipeline. GNA handles always-on speech recognition, acoustic noise suppression, speaker identification, and wake-word detection — all at sub-milliwatt power, with results fed directly to the Klangkerne for immediate audio response without CPU involvement.

  • Always-On TasksSpeech · Noise · Wake-Word · Speaker ID
  • CouplingDirect hardware path to Klangkerne
  • CPU OverheadZero — fully offloaded
  • ProcessIntel 14A-E
◆   Display Engine
Unified Display Controller · 14A-E
Eventide Display Engine
Unified Multi-GPU Display Controller · Intel 14A-E · No MUX Required

The Eventide Display Engine unifies all display output from every GPU tier — Elementalist, Druid, and 2DKR — through a single centralised controller tile. No GPU-to-GPU frame copies, no MUX switch, no awkward GPU ownership per display. Any GPU handles any display simultaneously: Wallpaper Engine on LPiGPU, desktop UI on 2DKR, and a game on HPiGPU — all concurrently, with zero contention.

The flagship output interface is iDP 1.0 (Intel Display Protocol, an in-house eDP derivative over PCIe 6.0 ×16×3), capable of 16K at 1200Hz on a single cable — the backbone of the Sony XCDLED display ecosystem. Codec signal transport (VRV1/VRV2) allows resolutions that would exceed PCIe 6.0 ×16×3 raw bandwidth to be carried compressed without quality loss.

  • ArchitectureUnified — no MUX, no GPU-GPU copy
  • iDP 1.0Up to 16K@1200Hz · PCIe 6.0 ×16×3
  • iDP Color DepthUp to 96-bit (RGB+MCY)
  • DisplayPortDP 2.1b UHBR20
  • eDPeDP 1.6
  • HDMIHDMI 2.2
  • Max Displays6 simultaneous native
  • iDP CountUp to 2× iDP ports
  • VRRFull VRR · XCD-LED DIAC · HDR10+ Ultra · Dolby Vision 2
  • Codec TransportVRV1 / VRV2 over iDP for >raw bandwidth resolutions
  • ProcessIntel 14A-E
16K

◆   Memory Subsystem
Memory Controller Tile · Lightmatter × Intel · FOveros-Mounted
ZAM Memory Controller
Zettascale Attached Memory Controllers · FOveros 2.0 Photonic Interconnect · Intel × Lightmatter

Eventide's memory subsystem is built around Lightmatter's photonic fabric and Intel's ZAM (Zettascale Attached Memory) architecture — a near-memory solution combining compute logic with DRAM stacks on the FOveros 2.0 base tile, delivering bandwidth fundamentally beyond conventional LPDDR or HBM solutions.

Base configurations ship with 2× dual-channel ZAM controllers for 5–6 TB/s effective bandwidth. Higher SKUs add a third DDR6/LPDDR6X dual-channel controller for capacity-prioritised workloads, bringing maximum unified memory to 2 TB. All tiles — CPU, GPU, NPU, audio, display — share this unified memory space via the photonic fabric, with the 4 GB Kache Kore™ bLLC acting as L4 cache to mask ZAM latency.

  • ZAM Controllers2× dual-channel (base) · 3× (higher SKU)
  • ZAM Bandwidth5–6 TB/s effective
  • ZAM Max CapacityUp to 1 TB ZAM
  • DDR6 ControllerDual-channel · DDR6-9600+ / LPDDR6X-10400
  • DDR6 Max CapacityUp to 1 TB DDR6
  • Max Unified MemoryUp to 2 TB (ZAM + DDR6)
  • bLLC (Kache Kore)4 GB L4 — masks ZAM latency
  • ECCYes — Xeon 500 class inline ECC
  • InterconnectLightmatter photonic FOveros 2.0 fabric
INTEL × LIGHTMATTER PHOTONIC FABRIC APU UNIFIED MEMORY
6
TB/s Bandwidth

TB Max Unified

GB bLLC Cache
ECC

◆   Connectivity Tiles
Wireless · Intel × OPPO/BBK · 14A-E
Killer SignalBoost S1
Intel® Killer™ × OPPO SignalBoost S1 · Intel 14A-E · Up to 2× chips

The result of Intel Killer's gaming-intelligence stack meeting OPPO Semiconductor's signal engineering expertise. The S1 delivers quad-band WiFi 7 per chip, with dual-chip configurations giving Eventide X500-class systems eight simultaneous frequency bands, 360° Intel LinkBoost antenna coverage, and an integrated 5G modem.

The OPPO-derived AI LinkBoost and the Intel Killer Prioritization Engine work in tandem: hardware-level gaming traffic prioritisation combined with dynamic multi-band signal optimisation — maintaining consistent <30ms gaming latency even under maximum network contention.

  • WiFi StandardWiFi 7 (802.11be) · Quad-band per chip
  • Max Configuration2× S1 — 8 total bands
  • Max ThroughputUp to 11.5 Gbps combined
  • Cellular5G Sub-6 + mmWave · 4G LTE · VoLTE
  • BluetoothBluetooth Core 6.2
  • SONAR PresenceWiFi-signal proximity detection · LPNPU-powered
  • ProcessIntel 14A-E
INTEL KILLER OPPO SEMICONDUCTOR BBK ELECTRONICS
8
WiFi bands (2× chip)
7
WiFi Generation
360°
LinkBoost Antenna
Platform I/O Controller · Intel 14A-E
I/O Controller
Thunderbolt 6 · PCIe 6.0 · USB4 Gen 5 · NVMe 3.0 · 2.5GbE · Intel 14A-E

The centralised I/O tile governing all wired external connectivity. Houses the Thunderbolt 6 controller (160 Gbps symmetric), the PCIe 6.0 root complex, USB4 Gen 5 (80 Gbps), NVMe 3.0 storage interface, and Intel 2.5GbE Ethernet — all coordinated from a single tile on Intel 14A-E. Thunderbolt 6's Bandwidth Boost mode delivers up to 240 Gbps unidirectional for display-intensive workloads, and PCIe 6.0 ×4 feeds NVMe at up to ~24 GB/s sequential read.

The TB6 controller carries Intel Need Technologies — the platform's QoS arbitration framework that prioritises latency-critical tunnels (display, audio) over PCIe/USB bulk transfers when bandwidth is contested.

  • ThunderboltThunderbolt 6 · 160 Gbps sym · 240 Gbps boost
  • TB6 PortsUp to 4× TB6 / USB4 Gen 5 ports
  • PCIePCIe 6.0 root complex (storage / peripherals)
  • NVMeNVMe 3.0 · PCIe 6.0 ×4 · ~24 GB/s
  • USBUSB4 Gen 5 (80 Gbps) · USB 3.2 Gen 2×2
  • EthernetIntel 2.5GbE on-package
  • TB6 Compat.Backward compat. TB4, TB5, USB4, USB-C
  • ProcessIntel 14A-E
160
Gbps TB6 (sym)
240
Gbps TB6 Boost
24
GB/s NVMe
◆   Platform Security & Management
Platform Security & Management · Intel 14A-E
PSM Tile
Intel® CSME 2.0 · Silicon Security Engine · Intel® EGP · Microsoft Pluton · vPro® · Intel 14A-E

Eventide's Platform Security & Management tile consolidates all hardware-rooted security, management, and power control functions onto a dedicated 14A-E tile — isolating the security domain entirely from compute paths.

The tile implements Intel's Eventide Guardian Protocol (EGP), an in-house alternative to Microsoft Pluton providing a silicon root of trust with on-tile key management, attestation, and anti-rollback firmware validation — purpose-built for the Eventide OS security model. Pluton 2.1 is also included for Windows ecosystem compatibility. The CSME 2.0 provides out-of-band management (Intel AMT), hardware DRM, and secure boot chain, while the Silicon Security Engine handles on-tile certification authority and FW signing.

  • In-House SecurityEventide Guardian Protocol (EGP)
  • EGP FunctionsSilicon RoT · On-tile key vault · Anti-rollback · Attestation
  • Microsoft PlutonPluton 2.1 · Hardware TPM 2.1
  • Intel CSMECSME 2.0 · AMT · Hardware DRM · Secure Boot
  • SSESilicon Security Engine · On-tile CA · FW signing
  • Intel vPro®Yes — Xeon 500 class; HW-rooted vPro
  • DMA ProtectionIntel VT-d · DMA remapping · CET shadow stack
  • Threat DetectionIntel TDT AI · MITRE ATT&CK / ATLAS mapped
  • Power ManagementPlatform PUNIT · all-tile sleep/wake sequencing
  • ProcessIntel 14A-E
Unified Platform Fabric
All tiles. One fabric. Zero boundaries.

Every tile in the Eventide Tile Mosaic is interconnected by the Lightmatter photonic FOveros 2.0 fabric — providing coherent, low-latency access to the ZAM unified memory pool from CPU, GPU, NPU, audio, display, and I/O domains simultaneously. No discrete architecture compromises. Full deep-dives on each tile follow.

TILE COUNT
12
DISTINCT SILICON DOMAINS
Product Family
One generation.
Four tiers. Zero compromise.

Eventide is a unique one-off generation — not a platform. Each tier is purpose-engineered for a specific use profile, from the ultra-portable Core 500 to the no-limits Core Xeon 500.

Intelligent Power Management
The Affinity System

Eventide's Affinity System is dynamic, not static. Profiles are generated in real-time by the Hardware Thread Director working in tandem with the system AI (LPNPU co-pilot model). The HW-TD enforces Affinity decisions in silicon — sub-millisecond transitions. The LPNPU predicts workload intent before it's scheduled, allowing the system to proactively shift between performance domains rather than reacting. Profiles can be tailored by users or third-party software, but the HW-TD and LPNPU continuously refine them at runtime.

⚡ V8 HyperBOOST, 4× GPU tiles, and All-Cores mode are available ONLY when connected to an External PSU + External Cooler

Core Xeon® 599HKX

The unconstrained ceiling of the Eventide generation. Four compute tiles. Four Arc E1080 iGPU tiles. Eight BionzXR transcoding cores. A fully populated Low Power Island. The world's most capable mobile APU.

880 Xe5-cores (450,560 shaders)
GPU Boost Clock
2.5 GHz
XMX5 AI units (total)
7,040 · ~67,830 AI TOPS combined (GPU)
RTU5 Ray Trace Units (total)
3,520
Multi-tile presentation
4× E1080 appear as single render device via FOveros 2.0 + Thread Director
VR Support
Yes — first Intel Arc gen. with native VR; VRV1 / VRV2 codec hardware
LP iGPU
Intel® Arc® Druid (Xe4E) — Intel 06E; always-on low-power 3D
Arc Druid Xe-cores
14 Xe4E-cores (3,584 shaders); RTX 5060M-class raster
2DKR Node
Intel 14A-E; dedicated 2D/GUI accelerator; no 3D ALUs; modernised Blitter Engine
Max Displays
6 (native)
Display Outputs
eDP 1.5, Intel×Sony eDP 2.0 (over PCIe 7.0 ×16×3), DisplayPort 2.1 UHBR20, HDMI 2.1 FRL
Max Resolution (eDP 2.0)
15360 × 9600 @ 1200 Hz
AI & Media ▼
HNPU (Intel 04A)
256 TOPS · INT2/4/8, FP8, BF16, FP16, FP32; 32 MB scratchpad SRAM
LPNPU (Intel 06E)
88 TOPS · <1.4W at peak; always-on; INT4/8, FP8, BF16
Total System AI TOPS
344 TOPS (NPU) + 67,830 TOPS (GPU XMX5) · coordinated via Thread Director
QuickSync BionzXR Cores
8 cores (Intel 06E) — co-developed with Sony Semiconductor
Encode Codecs (BionzXR)
AV2 (world-first), AV1, H.266/VVC, H.265, H.264, VRV1, VRV2, ProRes
Decode Codecs (BionzXR)
All encode codecs + VP9, VP8, MPEG-4, VC-1
Max Encode Resolution
8K120 ProRes; 8K240 AV1; 4K480 AV2
MFX QuickSync (14A-E)
Low-power decode: AV2, AV1, H.266, H.265, H.264, VP9 · −35% power vs PTL QuickSync
IPU Version
Intel IPU 8.2 (Intel 14A-E) — up to 201 MP; Sony USB-C/TB native
Intel® Gaussian & Neural Accelerator
Yes (GNA 5.0)
I/O & Connectivity ▼
PCIe Revision
PCIe 7.0 (primary GPU tile interconnect); PCIe 6.0 (storage / peripherals)
Thunderbolt Version
Thunderbolt 5 · 120 Gbps; supports Intel×Sony eDP 2.0 tunnel
USB Specification
USB4 Gen 4×2 (80 Gbps), USB 3.2 Gen 2×2
WiFi Specification
Intel® Killer™ Wi-Fi 7 (802.11be) — via Killer SignalBoost S1
WiFi Chips
Up to 2× X1 chips; quad-band each = 8 total bands
Bluetooth
Bluetooth Core 6.2
Cellular
5G Sub-6 / mmWave, 4G LTE, VoLTE
Antenna Support
Up to 2× 360° Intel LinkBoost antennae; SONAR user presence via WiFi signal
Intel® Wake on Voice
Yes (LPNPU-powered; always-on)
Intel® Wake on Wi-Fi (SONAR)
Yes
Security & Platform ▼
Intel® vPro®
Yes (Xeon 500 class; hardware-rooted vPro)
Microsoft Pluton
Yes — hardware TPM 2.1 + Pluton security processor (14A-E)
Intel® Thread Director
Yes — dedicated Thread Director tile; hardware-native 5-class scheduling
Thread Director Tile capabilities
Core class arbitration, tile sleep/wake, GPU/NPU escalation, thermal budget management, V8 HyperBOOST sequencing · LPNPU co-pilot: predictive AI-assisted task dispatch
Intel® Smart Sound Technology
Yes (14A-E)
Intel® High Definition Audio
Yes
Affinity Power System
Dynamic — Performance / Balanced / Efficiency / Unleashed; hardware-enforced via Thread Director + LPNPU AI co-pilot · Unleashed requires external PSU + cooler
TDP (Base / Max Turbo)
55W base cTDP / Up to 175W sustained (HKX OC mode)
Min LP Island TDP
1W (LPE-only; efficiency affinity; Compute Tile off)
Processor Graphics TDP
Configurable per-tile; min 3W (Druid standby); max 4×75W (4× E1080 full)
